title: Track runtime pointer entropy
overview: Create a pointer tracking module at src/input/pointer.ts that captures normalized mouse and touch position/velocity in viewport space, exposes a readable snapshot for the render loop, and persists nothing to storage.

steps.action: Create src/input/pointer.ts with PointerState interface and tracking logic
details: Define PointerState interface: { x: number, y: number, dx: number, dy: number, speed: number, active: boolean } — all values normalized to 0–1 viewport space (dx/dy as fractions of viewport per frame)
Export initPointer(target: HTMLElement): { getState(): PointerState, destroy(): void }
Listen to 'pointermove' on target (covers both mouse and touch via Pointer Events API)
On each pointermove: compute normalized x/y (clientX/innerWidth, clientY/innerHeight), derive dx/dy from previous position, compute speed as sqrt(dx²+dy²)
Track 'active' via pointerenter/pointerleave (or pointerdown/pointerup for touch)
getState() returns a frozen snapshot of the latest values — no history array, no persistence
destroy() removes all event listeners (returns cleanup)
Default PointerState when no movement yet: { x: 0.5, y: 0.5, dx: 0, dy: 0, speed: 0, active: false }
Import initPointer from src/input/pointer.ts
Call initPointer(canvas) after initScene, before startLoop
No need to pass to render loop yet — render loop integration is a future story (mappings). Just initialize so tracking is live.
Test: pointermove dispatched on target updates x/y to normalized viewport coordinates
Test: dx/dy reflect difference between consecutive moves
Test: speed is computed as magnitude of (dx, dy)
Test: touch input works (dispatch PointerEvent with pointerType 'touch')
Test: getState() returns default values before any movement
Test: destroy() removes listeners (dispatch after destroy, state unchanged)
Test: no localStorage, sessionStorage, cookies, or IndexedDB usage (privacy check — pattern from existing scaffold.privacy.test.ts)
Test: coordinates are clamped/normalized to 0–1 range even at viewport edges
Run full test suite: npx vitest run
Run build: npx vite build
Confirm no console errors, no storage API usage

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Pointer velocity or delta is derived: Step 1 — dx/dy and speed computed from consecutive events
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Mouse and touch both work: Step 1 — Pointer Events API unifies mouse and touch; test with pointerType 'touch'
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.No pointer history is persisted: Step 1 — only latest snapshot kept, no array/storage; Step 3 — privacy test

designNotes: Uses Pointer Events API (not separate mouse/touch listeners) for unified input handling — well-supported in all modern browsers
Normalized 0–1 coordinate space decouples pointer data from viewport size, matching CLAUDE.md guidance to normalize to viewport space
No history buffer: only the latest frame's state is stored in a single object, satisfying the no-persistence criterion
Module follows the same pattern as signals.ts: pure function, typed interface, no side effects at import time
The destroy() cleanup pattern matches attachResizeHandler's existing convention
risks: Pointer Events may need { passive: true } for touch to avoid jank — add this to addEventListener options
High-frequency pointermove events on desktop could be noisy — consider whether to apply any smoothing (defer unless needed, keep simple for now)
